description: Set the absolute position of a container on the canvas using logical row and column coordinates.
utility: Position containers at absolute logical row/col coordinates on the canvas

arguments.name: row
arguments.description: Vertical position in logical units (0 = top, increases downward)

arguments.name: col
arguments.description: Horizontal position in logical units (0 = left, increases rightward)

alternates.description: Place a 2D graph at row, col
Place a 3D scene at row, col
Place written math at row, col
Place a table at row, col
Place an input dropdown at row, col
Two graphs side by side using different col values
Two graphs stacked using different row values

examples.scenario: Graph with axes positioned with offset
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(5,26), 13.3, 20.6, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 5, 1), grid(noaxes), type(plotting))

examples.scenario: Write math text at specific position

examples.expression: t = table(at(1,7), "x", eq, slopeeq, range(2, 1.5, 1.1, 1.01, 1.001), c(blue))

examples.scenario: Grid of 6 graphs (2 rows x 3 cols)
examples.expression:
G1 = g2d(at(2,2), 14, 14, type(plotting))
G2 = g2d(at(2,18), 14, 14, type(plotting))
G3 = g2d(at(2,34), 14, 14, type(plotting))
G4 = g2d(at(18,2), 14, 14, type(plotting))
G5 = g2d(at(18,18), 14, 14, type(plotting))
G6 = g2d(at(18,34), 14, 14, type(plotting))

G = g2d(at(2,3), 14, 14, type(plotting))
write_1 = write(at(18,3), "f(x) = x^2")

eq1 = input(at(0,2), value("a*x^2 + b*x + c", "a*sin(b*x)"), "f(x)")
G1 = g2d(at(2,2), 14, 14)
